title: Podman container fails to start when both static IPv6 address and SELinux label are specified
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

When both static IPv6 address and SELinux label are specified together, the container fails to start. When the SELinux label option is removed, the container starts without issues. Or, when IPv6 is changed to IPv4, then it works too.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a mavclan interface with IPv6 address
` podman network create -d macvlan --subnet "2001:db8:dead:babe::/64" --gateway "2001:db8:dead:babe::1" -o parent=ens224 ens224`

2. Start a container with static IPv6 address and SELinux label
`/usr/bin/podman run -dt -i=true --security-opt label=level:s0:c2001:db8:dead:babe::4 --network ens224 --ip 2001:db8:dead:babe::4 --name mycontainer myimage:latest`
or
`/usr/bin/podman run -dt -i=true --security-opt label=level:s0:c2001:db8:dead:babe::4 --network ens224 --ip6 2001:db8:dead:babe::4 --name mycontainer myimage:latest`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: failed to mount shm tmpfs "/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/79be4480bd12d33da3ce2fe703e696ca81f394f01bdae06ea6440575355911d0/userdata/shm": invalid argument
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
The container should get started

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
When both podman run commands are run without `--security-opt` option, the container comes up fine.


**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.0.0-dev

Go Version:   go1.17.5
Git Commit:   6ffd59828dd3c58c0927bd715e0a5a7b0c3baf2f

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

RHEL 8.5 VM

comments.body: `--security-opt label=level:s0:c2001:db8:dead:babe::4` is not a valid selinux label. I assume you only want `--security-opt label=level:s0`
My bad. I was using an automated script and didn't pay attention to that. Thanks @Luap99
